1.Joe MackLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.209 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.372 (-106 pts)K 26.4%BB 5.7%HR 0.0%Hit 15.2%Strong Pitcher

Joe Mack LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 133+0+0, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Joe Mack should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.209 xwOBA (-106 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -87 pts (bb_type-adj 0.296 -> final 0.209); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

4-Seam Fastball-4.4+10.39+5.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.221 / xBA 0.273 / xSLG 0.455 (-94 pts)K 19.2%BB 9.1%HR 5.0%Hit 12.3%Edge Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 164+12+189, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.221 xwOBA (-94 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -124 pts (bb_type-adj 0.345 -> final 0.221); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-77 pts vs season). Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+4.518-0.2on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.James OutmanLHBMIN vs Robert Gasser (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.241 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.404 (-74 pts)K 24.8%BB 11.6%HR 1.6%Hit 14.9%Edge Pitcher

James Outman LHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 163+605+601, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while James Outman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.241 xwOBA (-74 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -71 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.241); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 571+1867+2170, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.250 xwOBA (-65 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -36 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.246); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 70.1 mph
swing length 7.2 ft
attack angle 4.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+2.3175-2.4on plane
Sweeper-6.9+7.755+0.8on plane
Sinker-6.1+1.3126-4.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Split-Finger-7.1+9.910+2.8on plane
Slider-6.6+7.2114+0.6on plane
Changeup-7.3+9.717+2.4on plane
Curveball-8.3+7.429-0.9on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

5.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.254 / xBA 0.206 / xSLG 0.322 (-61 pts)K 22.8%BB 3.8%HR 2.4%Hit 19.5%Edge Pitcher

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 345+834+1141, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.254 xwOBA (-61 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.262); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.262 -> final 0.254); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Vázquez is on a 14-day slump (-51 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 779+0+0, pitcher rows 820+3482+2959

Verdict

Projection: 0.262 xwOBA (-53 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.299 -> final 0.262); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-55 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Sinker-6.0+12.318+6.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

8.Justin CrawfordLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.265 / xBA 0.208 / xSLG 0.382 (-50 pts)K 28.2%BB 6.3%HR 1.9%Hit 20.0%Edge Pitcher

Justin Crawford LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 510+0+0, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Crawford should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.265 xwOBA (-50 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.293 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.286 -> final 0.265); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

9.J.T. RealmutoRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.265 / xBA 0.204 / xSLG 0.309 (-50 pts)K 28.4%BB 2.8%HR 1.8%Hit 21.7%Slight Pitcher

J.T. Realmuto RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 367+2264+1662, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while J.T. Realmuto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.265 xwOBA (-50 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.250); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.250 -> final 0.265); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

11.Maverick HandleyRHBBAL vs Miles Mikolas (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.269 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.420 (-46 pts)K 17.2%BB 3.8%HR 0.0%Hit 27.6%Slight Pitcher

Maverick Handley RHB vs Miles Mikolas RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 0+183+0, pitcher rows 624+2487+2705

Verdict

Mikolas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Maverick Handley should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.269 xwOBA (-46 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.312); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -43 pts (bb_type-adj 0.312 -> final 0.269); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Isaac Paredes RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 733+1944+2862, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Isaac Paredes should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.274 xwOBA (-41 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.294 -> adj 0.279); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Split-Finger30.00.3010.276GB 65% / LD 15% / FB 14% / PU 6%0.279-220.2400.1890.26744.1
4-Seam Fastball27.30.3240.296GB 58% / LD 21% / FB 17% / PU 4%0.321-30.3030.2520.3425.0

Slider3.40.2960.322GB 57% / LD 19% / FB 20% / PU 5%0.276-200.2830.2320.35015.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Rasmussen's cutter is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). 14.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.277 / xBA 0.211 / xSLG 0.308 (-38 pts)K 27.8%BB 4.2%HR 4.3%Hit 18.8%Slight Pitcher

Christian Walker RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 693+2519+2326, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.277 xwOBA (-38 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.261); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.261 -> final 0.277); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-120 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 16.Liam HicksLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.362 (-36 pts)K 16.8%BB 7.0%HR 2.5%Hit 20.9%Slight Pitcher

Liam Hicks LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 565+1537+0, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Liam Hicks should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Liam Hicks is on a 14-day slump (-64 pts vs season). Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

17.Joey WiemerRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.407 (-36 pts)K 26.7%BB 6.2%HR 2.5%Hit 23.4%Slight Pitcher

Verdict

Young's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Joey Wiemer should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.303 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

18.Darell HernaizRHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.349 (-35 pts)K 13.2%BB 4.2%HR 1.2%Hit 26.3%Slight Pitcher

Darell Hernaiz RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 308+712+511, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.298 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

19.Trea TurnerRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.222 / xSLG 0.340 (-34 pts)K 26.1%BB 2.5%HR 2.4%Hit 24.1%Slight Pitcher

Trea Turner RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 722+2484+2122, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.281 -> adj 0.269); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.269 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 20.Jhonny PeredaRHBSEA vs Lucas Giolito (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.395 (-33 pts)K 18.7%BB 8.7%HR 0.0%Hit 22.2%Slight Pitcher

Jhonny Pereda RHB vs Lucas Giolito RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 31+333+158, pitcher rows 0+2372+0

Verdict

Giolito's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jhonny Pereda should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.308 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

21.Adolis GarcíaRHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.222 / xSLG 0.332 (-33 pts)K 30.2%BB 2.6%HR 3.5%Hit 22.5%Slight Pitcher

Adolis García RHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 719+2154+2542, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Adolis García should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.283 -> adj 0.266); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.266 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adolis García is on a 14-day slump (-52 pts vs season). Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

22.Alejandro OsunaLHBTEX vs Peter Lambert (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.358 (-33 pts)K 21.8%BB 14.5%HR 0.3%Hit 17.4%Slight Pitcher

Alejandro Osuna LHB vs Peter Lambert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 193+744+0, pitcher rows 478+0+1044

Verdict

Lambert's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Alejandro Osuna should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

23.Xavier EdwardsLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.283 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.358 (-32 pts)K 16.9%BB 7.0%HR 1.0%Hit 22.6%Slight Pitcher

Xavier Edwards LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 735+2260+1192, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.283); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

24.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Robert Gasser (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.283 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.384 (-32 pts)K 10.3%BB 4.6%HR 1.0%Hit 22.2%Slight Pitcher

Austin Martin RHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 544+749+934, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.283); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Austin Martin is on a 14-day slump (-47 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

25.Zach ColeLHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.283 / xBA 0.266 / xSLG 0.380 (-32 pts)K 26.7%BB 3.4%HR 5.6%Hit 19.4%Slight Pitcher

Zach Cole LHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 122+224+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Cole should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -25 pts (bb_type-adj 0.308 -> final 0.283); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

27.Brandon MarshLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.287 / xBA 0.213 / xSLG 0.354 (-28 pts)K 30.0%BB 5.9%HR 2.1%Hit 23.1%Slight Pitcher

Brandon Marsh LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 538+1724+2062, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Marsh should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.287 xwOBA (-28 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.274); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.274 -> final 0.287); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

28.Vaughn GrissomRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.287 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.424 (-28 pts)K 15.5%BB 7.3%HR 4.8%Hit 17.6%Slight Pitcher

Vaughn Grissom RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 350+0+444, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Vaughn Grissom should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.287 xwOBA (-28 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.287); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Vaughn Grissom is on a 14-day heater (+44 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

29.Cam SmithRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.288 / xBA 0.213 / xSLG 0.339 (-27 pts)K 29.5%BB 4.8%HR 1.6%Hit 23.8%Slight Pitcher

Cam Smith RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 708+2025+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Cam Smith should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.288 xwOBA (-27 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.275); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.275 -> final 0.288); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Sal Frelick LHB vs Bailey Ober RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 587+2436+2087, pitcher rows 776+2356+2697

Verdict

Ober's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Frelick should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.294 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Bailey Ober is in a 14-day hot stretch (-77 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Chandler Simpson LHB vs Eury Pérez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 578+1518+0, pitcher rows 803+1675+0

Verdict

Pérez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Chandler Simpson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. 32.Jacob YoungRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.292 / xBA 0.223 / xSLG 0.380 (-23 pts)K 19.2%BB 4.7%HR 0.3%Hit 27.7%Even

Jacob Young RHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+1368+1956, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jacob Young should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.292 xwOBA (-23 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jacob Young is on a 14-day slump (-41 pts vs season). Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

33.Matt ShawRHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.385 (-22 pts)K 16.8%BB 7.5%HR 1.3%Hit 21.4%Even

Matt Shaw RHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 373+1804+0, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Shaw should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

34.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.463 (-22 pts)K 22.1%BB 7.8%HR 5.8%Hit 19.5%Even

Eric Haase RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+339+270, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.342); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -49 pts (bb_type-adj 0.342 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 36.Keibert RuizRHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.384 (-21 pts)K 15.1%BB 3.4%HR 2.5%Hit 24.9%Even

Keibert Ruiz RHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 279+889+1662, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Keibert Ruiz should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

37.Maikel GarciaRHBKC vs Andre Pallante (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.295 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.360 (-20 pts)K 10.9%BB 8.1%HR 1.3%Hit 23.5%Even

Maikel Garcia RHB vs Andre Pallante RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 722+2521+2434, pitcher rows 728+2722+1981

Verdict

Pallante's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Maikel Garcia should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.295 xwOBA (-20 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

38.Zack GelofRHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.354 (-18 pts)K 21.7%BB 4.0%HR 2.5%Hit 25.1%Even

Zack Gelof RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 320+416+2056, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Zack Gelof is on a 14-day heater (+44 pts vs season). Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

39.Sebastián RiveroRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.383 (-18 pts)K 18.8%BB 6.4%HR 0.0%Hit 26.8%Even

Sebastián Rivero RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 120+127+0, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Sebastián Rivero should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.317 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

40.Victor CaratiniRHBMIN vs Robert Gasser (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.298 / xBA 0.223 / xSLG 0.373 (-17 pts)K 13.3%BB 2.8%HR 2.9%Hit 7.5%Even

Victor Caratini RHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 427+1418+1073, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Victor Caratini should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.298 xwOBA (-17 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.292 -> final 0.298); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

42.Andruw MonasterioRHBBOS vs Grant Holmes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.299 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.370 (-16 pts)K 27.3%BB 7.3%HR 2.1%Hit 21.7%Even

Andruw Monasterio RHB vs Grant Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 313+563+647, pitcher rows 656+1970+1029

Verdict

Holmes' slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Andruw Monasterio should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.299 xwOBA (-16 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.292); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.292 -> final 0.299); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 66.0 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 9.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-8.6+16.628+7.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Split-Finger-7.7+2.3*0-5.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-6.9+2.3*3-4.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Cutter-5.3+1.17-4.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

44.Elias DíazRHBKC vs Andre Pallante (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.300 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.359 (-15 pts)K 17.7%BB 7.4%HR 3.4%Hit 21.0%Even

Elias Díaz RHB vs Andre Pallante RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+1056+1337, pitcher rows 728+2722+1981

Verdict

Pallante's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Elias Díaz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.300); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Jonah Heim RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 201+1704+1783, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jonah Heim should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.300); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

46.Bryson StottLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.300 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.371 (-15 pts)K 24.6%BB 6.0%HR 2.1%Hit 24.3%Even

Bryson Stott LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 561+2522+2375, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' split-finger is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.300 xwOBA (-15 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.283 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.300); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Myles StrawRHBTOR vs Jack Flaherty (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.301 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.401 (-14 pts)K 23.7%BB 6.1%HR 2.5%Hit 24.6%Even

Myles Straw RHB vs Jack Flaherty RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 301+1178+14, pitcher rows 786+3028+3034

Verdict

Flaherty's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Myles Straw should look to drive the knuckle curve. Projection: 0.301 xwOBA (-14 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.301); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Pallante's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Isbel should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.301 xwOBA (-14 pts vs league, Pitcher). Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

50.Colby ThomasRHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.317 (-13 pts)K 19.2%BB 3.3%HR 0.0%Hit 30.5%Even

Colby Thomas RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 105+481+0, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -28 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.270); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +31 pts (bb_type-adj 0.270 -> final 0.302); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Curveball6.90.3120.308GB 56% / LD 20% / FB 18% / PU 6%0.312+00.3050.2400.389100.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).
